mission.title: Install and Configure Piraeus Datastore on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Piraeus Operator manages LINSTOR clusters in Kubernetes, providing a robust solution for software-defined storage. Installing it allows you to easily deploy and manage storage resources in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Piraeus Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add piraeus https://charts.piraeus.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Piraeus Operator
steps.description:
Install the Piraeus Operator using Helm with the specified version:
```bash
helm install piraeus piraeus/piraeus --version 2.10.4 --namespace piraeus-datastore --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Piraeus Operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl wait pod --for=condition=Ready -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/component=piraeus-operator
```

steps.title: Create a LINSTOR cluster
steps.description:
Deploy a basic LINSTOR cluster by applying the following manifest:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: piraeus.io/v1
kind: LinstorCluster
metadata:
  name: linstorcluster
spec: {}
EOF
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Helm release to set resource limits for the operator:
```bash
helm upgrade piraeus piraeus/piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore --set operator.resources.limits.cpu=100m --set operator.resources.limits.memory=128Mi
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the operator
steps.description:
Set up port-forwarding to access the operator's API:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/piraeus-operator-controller-manager -n piraeus-datastore 8080:443
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Piraeus Operator release:
```bash
helm uninstall piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions associated with the Piraeus Operator:
```bash
kubectl delete crd linstorclusters.piraeus.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Piraeus Operator:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace piraeus-datastore
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current LINSTOR cluster configuration:
```bash
kubectl get linstorcluster -n piraeus-datastore -o yaml > linstorcluster-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Piraeus Helm repository to get the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Piraeus Operator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Piraeus Operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade piraeus piraeus/piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore --version 2.10.4
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the upgraded Piraeus Operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl wait pod --for=condition=Ready -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/component=piraeus-operator
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This may indicate a configuration issue. Check the logs of the pod:
```bash
kubectl logs -n piraeus-datastore <pod-name>
```
Look for error messages that indicate what went wrong.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n piraeus-datastore
```
Check the endpoints:
```bash
kubectl get endpoints -n piraeus-datastore
```

troubleshooting.title: Failed to create LinstorCluster
troubleshooting.description:
If the LinstorCluster fails to create, check the events:
```bash
kubectl describe linstorcluster linstorcluster -n piraeus-datastore
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are not starting due to resource limits, check the resource usage:
```bash
kubectl top pods -n piraeus-datastore
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of the Piraeus Operator will have the operator pod running in the 'piraeus-datastore' namespace, and you will be able to create a LINSTOR cluster. You can verify this by checking the pod status and ensuring no errors are present in the logs.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add piraeus https://charts.piraeus.io
helm repo update

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: piraeus.io/v1
kind: LinstorCluster
metadata:
  name: linstorcluster
spec: {}
EOF
helm upgrade piraeus piraeus/piraeus --namespace piraeus-datastore --set operator.resources.limits.cpu=100m --set operator.resources.limits.memory=128Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and Helm installed.
